children.title: You said alright thats all my information. sorry i didnt read your previous messages. can you summarize what you said with all of the info.

children.value: alright thats all my information. sorry i didnt read your previous messages. can you summarize what you said with all of the info.

i'm following. based on your spending and the details of your cards, your most effective strategy is to use the capital one venture x as your primary card for almost everything. since you spend over $4,400 on home and utilities and have a large amount of uncategorized spending, the flat 2x miles on the venture x will earn you much more than the 1% you would get on your other cards for those items. your bank of america customized cash card is best used for a specific 3% category, and i recommend setting it to online shopping. this will cover your shopping and entertainment expenses and likely capture some of your digital startup costs or uncategorized online bills. however, this 3% rate is capped at $2,500 in spending per quarter. once you spend that amount, you should move all transactions back to the venture x to avoid dropping to 1% rewards. the $956 you have in bank of america rewards is substantial, and if you have at least $20,000 in a bank of america or merrill account, you may be eligible for preferred rewards which would boost that 3% rate even higher. your amex delta blue is your weakest card for daily spending because it earns delta miles instead of flexible points, so you should only use it when you have manually activated a specific offer for a merchant like starbucks or uber. the error you saw on the amex site is simply because your card earns skymiles rather than membership rewards points.
finally, since you have the venture x, you should pay your monthly cell phone bill with it to take advantage of the $800 in protection coverage mentioned in your benefits guide.

you have $956 waiting in bofa rewards. if you have $20k+ in your accounts there, you might be eligible for a "preferred rewards" boost to earn even more.

children.title: You said wait so amex is exclusive to certain rewards?

children.value: wait so amex is exclusive to certain rewards?
i hear you. yes, amex rewards are siloed based on the type of card you have.

children.title: the two main amex reward worlds

children.value: the two main amex reward worlds
the reason the amex site told you that you aren't "authorized" for membership rewards is simply because your card doesn't participate in that program. it only speaks "delta."
even though you can't get membership rewards, you still have access to
. these are the merchant deals you saw for uber and starbucks. these pay out in
(cash back to your bill), so they are independent of whether you earn delta miles or amex points.

if you find yourself wanting those flexible membership rewards points in the future, you would have to open a separate amex card that isn't tied to a specific airline. for now, treat the delta blue as a "coupon card" for those specific merchant offers.

children.title: You said should i select my bofa deals

children.value: should i select my bofa deals
yes, you should absolutely select them, but only the ones for places where you already spend money.

there is no downside to activating them. these deals (bankamerideals) are essentially digital coupons that link to your card. when you spend at a participating merchant, the cash back is automatically credited to your rewards balance, usually within a few weeks.
since you are a regular at starbucks and often use uber, check specifically for those. based on your spending habits, keep an eye out for:

none, other than the fact that they expire. you have to manually click "add to card" for each one before you make the purchase. it takes about thirty seconds once a week to scroll through and add the ones you might use. since you have $956 in rewards sitting there, these deals are a low-effort way to keep that number growing.

children.title: You said should i use delta or bofa for uber and stuff.

children.value: should i use delta or bofa for uber and stuff.
i'm following. the choice between the two depends entirely on which specific "deal" or "offer" you have active at the moment.

the only "exclusive" thing about the amex delta blue is the ability to earn delta miles and access those merchant offers. it's rarely your best card for daily spending unless a specific offer is active.
